member.name: M:Wolverine.AzureServiceBus.AzureServiceBusConfiguration.ConfigureQueue(System.String,System.Action{Azure.Messaging.ServiceBus.Administration.CreateQueueOptions})
member.summary: Add explicit configuration to an AzureServiceBus queue that is being created by this application

member.name: M:Wolverine.AzureServiceBus.AzureServiceBusConfiguration.UseTopicAndSubscriptionConventionalRouting(System.Action{Wolverine.AzureServiceBus.AzureServiceBusTopicBroadcastingRoutingConvention})
member.summary: Opt into using conventional message routing using topics and topic subscriptions based on message type names

member.name: M:Wolverine.AzureServiceBus.AzureServiceBusConfiguration.UseConventionalRouting(System.Action{Wolverine.AzureServiceBus.AzureServiceBusMessageRoutingConvention})
member.summary: Opt into using conventional message routing using queues based on message type names

member.name: M:Wolverine.AzureServiceBus.AzureServiceBusConfiguration.SystemQueuesAreEnabled(System.Boolean)
member.summary: Is Wolverine enabled to create system queues automatically for responses and retries? This should probably be set to false if the application does not have permissions to create queues

member.name: M:Wolverine.AzureServiceBus.AzureServiceBusMessageRoutingConvention.QueueNameForSender(System.Func{System.Type,System.String})
member.summary: Specify naming rules for the subscribing queue for message types

member.name: M:Wolverine.AzureServiceBus.AzureServiceBusQueueListenerConfiguration.CircuitBreaker(System.Action{Wolverine.ErrorHandling.CircuitBreakerOptions})
member.summary: Add circuit breaker exception handling to this listener

member.name: M:Wolverine.AzureServiceBus.AzureServiceBusQueueListenerConfiguration.ConfigureQueue(System.Action{Azure.Messaging.ServiceBus.Administration.CreateQueueOptions})
member.summary: Configure the underlying Azure Service Bus queue. This is only applicable when Wolverine is creating the queues

member.name: M:Wolverine.AzureServiceBus.AzureServiceBusQueueListenerConfiguration.MaximumMessagesToReceive(System.Int32)
member.summary: The maximum number of messages to receive in a single batch when listening in either buffered or durable modes. The default is 20.

member.name: M:Wolverine.AzureServiceBus.AzureServiceBusQueueListenerConfiguration.MaximumWaitTime(System.TimeSpan)
member.summary: The duration for which the listener waits for a message to arrive in the queue before returning. If a message is available, the call returns sooner than this time. If no messages are available and the wait time expires, the call returns successfully with an empty list of messages. Default is 5 seconds.

member.name: M:Wolverine.AzureServiceBus.AzureServiceBusQueueListenerConfiguration.DisableDeadLetterQueueing
member.summary: Completely disable all SQS dead letter queueing for just this queue

member.name: M:Wolverine.AzureServiceBus.AzureServiceBusQueueListenerConfiguration.ConfigureDeadLetterQueue(System.String,System.Action{Wolverine.AzureServiceBus.Internal.AzureServiceBusQueue})
member.summary: Customize the dead letter queueing for just this queue
member.param: Optionally configure properties of the dead letter queue itself

member.name: M:Wolverine.AzureServiceBus.AzureServiceBusQueueListenerConfiguration.RequireSessions(System.Nullable{System.Int32})
member.summary: Force this queue to require session identifiers. Use this for FIFO semantics
member.param: The maximum number of parallel sessions that can be processed at any one time

member.name: M:Wolverine.AzureServiceBus.AzureServiceBusQueueListenerConfiguration.InteropWith(Wolverine.AzureServiceBus.IAzureServiceBusEnvelopeMapper)
member.summary: Utilize custom envelope mapping for Amazon Service Bus interoperability with external non-Wolverine systems

member.name: M:Wolverine.AzureServiceBus.AzureServiceBusQueueSubscriberConfiguration.ConfigureQueue(System.Action{Azure.Messaging.ServiceBus.Administration.CreateQueueOptions})
member.summary: Configure the underlying Azure Service Bus queue. This is only applicable when Wolverine is creating the queues

member.name: M:Wolverine.AzureServiceBus.AzureServiceBusQueueSubscriberConfiguration.RequireSessions(System.Nullable{System.Int32})
member.summary: Force this queue to require session identifiers. Use this for FIFO semantics
member.param: The maximum number of parallel sessions that can be processed at any one time

member.name: M:Wolverine.AzureServiceBus.AzureServiceBusQueueSubscriberConfiguration.InteropWith(Wolverine.AzureServiceBus.IAzureServiceBusEnvelopeMapper)
member.summary: Utilize custom envelope mapping for Amazon Service Bus interoperability with external non-Wolverine systems

member.name: M:Wolverine.AzureServiceBus.AzureServiceBusSubscriptionListenerConfiguration.CircuitBreaker(System.Action{Wolverine.ErrorHandling.CircuitBreakerOptions})
member.summary: Add circuit breaker exception handling to this listener

member.name: M:Wolverine.AzureServiceBus.AzureServiceBusSubscriptionListenerConfiguration.ConfigureSubscription(System.Action{Azure.Messaging.ServiceBus.Administration.CreateSubscriptionOptions})
member.summary: Configure the underlying Azure Service Bus Subscription. This is only applicable when Wolverine is creating the Subscriptions

member.name: M:Wolverine.AzureServiceBus.AzureServiceBusSubscriptionListenerConfiguration.ConfigureTopic(System.Action{Azure.Messaging.ServiceBus.Administration.CreateTopicOptions})
member.summary: Configure the underlying Azure Service Bus Subscription. This is only applicable when Wolverine is creating the Subscriptions

member.name: M:Wolverine.AzureServiceBus.AzureServiceBusSubscriptionListenerConfiguration.MaximumMessagesToReceive(System.Int32)
member.summary: The maximum number of messages to receive in a single batch when listening in either buffered or durable modes. The default is 20.

member.name: M:Wolverine.AzureServiceBus.AzureServiceBusSubscriptionListenerConfiguration.MaximumWaitTime(System.TimeSpan)
member.summary: The duration for which the listener waits for a message to arrive in the Subscription before returning. If a message is available, the call returns sooner than this time. If no messages are available and the wait time expires, the call returns successfully with an empty list of messages. Default is 5 seconds.

member.name: M:Wolverine.AzureServiceBus.AzureServiceBusSubscriptionListenerConfiguration.RequireSessions(System.Nullable{System.Int32})
member.summary: Force this subscription listener to require session identifiers. Use this for FIFO semantics
member.param: The maximum number of parallel sessions that can be processed at any one time

member.name: M:Wolverine.AzureServiceBus.AzureServiceBusSubscriptionListenerConfiguration.InteropWith(Wolverine.AzureServiceBus.IAzureServiceBusEnvelopeMapper)
member.summary: Utilize custom envelope mapping for Amazon Service Bus interoperability with external non-Wolverine systems

member.name: M:Wolverine.AzureServiceBus.AzureServiceBusTopicBroadcastingRoutingConvention.TopicNameForSender(System.Func{System.Type,System.String})
member.summary: Override the naming convention for topics. Identical in functionality to IdentifierForSender()

member.name: M:Wolverine.AzureServiceBus.AzureServiceBusTopicBroadcastingRoutingConvention.SubscriptionNameForListener(System.Func{System.Type,System.String})
member.summary: Override the subscription name for a message type. By default this would be the same as the topic

member.name: M:Wolverine.AzureServiceBus.AzureServiceBusTopicBroadcastingRoutingConvention.TopicNameForListener(System.Func{System.Type,System.String})
member.summary: Override the topic name by message type for listeners. This has the same functionality as IdentifierForListener()

member.name: M:Wolverine.AzureServiceBus.AzureServiceBusTopicSubscriberConfiguration.ConfigureTopic(System.Action{Azure.Messaging.ServiceBus.Administration.CreateTopicOptions})
member.summary: Configure the underlying Azure Service Bus Topic. This is only applicable when Wolverine is creating the Topics

member.name: M:Wolverine.AzureServiceBus.AzureServiceBusTopicSubscriberConfiguration.InteropWith(Wolverine.AzureServiceBus.IAzureServiceBusEnvelopeMapper)
member.summary: Utilize custom envelope mapping for Amazon Service Bus interoperability with external non-Wolverine systems

member.name: P:Wolverine.AzureServiceBus.AzureServiceBusTransport.SystemQueuesEnabled
member.summary: Is this transport connection allowed to build and use response and retry queues for just this node?

member.name: M:Wolverine.AzureServiceBus.AzureServiceBusTransportExtensions.AzureServiceBusTransport(Wolverine.WolverineOptions)
member.summary: Quick access to the Rabbit MQ Transport within this application. This is for advanced usage

member.name: M:Wolverine.AzureServiceBus.AzureServiceBusTransportExtensions.UseAzureServiceBus(Wolverine.WolverineOptions,System.String,System.Action{Azure.Messaging.ServiceBus.ServiceBusClientOptions})
member.summary: Connect to Azure Service Bus with a connection string

member.name: M:Wolverine.AzureServiceBus.AzureServiceBusTransportExtensions.UseAzureServiceBus(Wolverine.WolverineOptions,System.String,Azure.Core.TokenCredential,System.Action{Azure.Messaging.ServiceBus.ServiceBusClientOptions})
member.summary: Connect to Azure Service Bus using a namespace and secured through a TokenCredential

member.name: M:Wolverine.AzureServiceBus.AzureServiceBusTransportExtensions.UseAzureServiceBus(Wolverine.WolverineOptions,System.String,Azure.AzureNamedKeyCredential,System.Action{Azure.Messaging.ServiceBus.ServiceBusClientOptions})
member.summary: Connect to Azure Service Bus using a namespace and secured through an AzureNamedKeyCredential

member.name: M:Wolverine.AzureServiceBus.AzureServiceBusTransportExtensions.UseAzureServiceBus(Wolverine.WolverineOptions,System.String,Azure.AzureSasCredential,System.Action{Azure.Messaging.ServiceBus.ServiceBusClientOptions})
member.summary: Connect to Azure Service Bus using a namespace and secured through an AzureSasCredential

member.name: M:Wolverine.AzureServiceBus.AzureServiceBusTransportExtensions.ListenToAzureServiceBusSubscription(Wolverine.WolverineOptions,System.String,System.Action{Azure.Messaging.ServiceBus.Administration.CreateSubscriptionOptions})
member.summary: Listen for messages from an Azure Service Bus topic subscription
member.param: Optionally apply customizations to the Azure Service Bus topic
Optionally apply customizations to the actual Azure Service Bus subscription

member.name: M:Wolverine.AzureServiceBus.AzureServiceBusTransportExtensions.ToAzureServiceBusQueue(Wolverine.Configuration.IPublishToExpression,System.String)
member.summary: Publish the designated messages directly to an Azure Service Bus queue

member.name: M:Wolverine.AzureServiceBus.AzureServiceBusTransportExtensions.ToAzureServiceBusTopic(Wolverine.Configuration.IPublishToExpression,System.String)
member.summary: Publish the designated messages to an Azure Service Bus topic

member.name: P:Wolverine.AzureServiceBus.Internal.IAzureServiceBusListeningEndpoint.MaximumMessagesToReceive
member.summary: The maximum number of messages to receive in a single batch when listening in either buffered or durable modes. The default is 20.

member.name: P:Wolverine.AzureServiceBus.Internal.IAzureServiceBusListeningEndpoint.MaximumWaitTime
member.summary: The duration for which the listener waits for a message to arrive in the queue before returning. If a message is available, the call returns sooner than this time. If no messages are available and the wait time expires, the call returns successfully with an empty list of messages. Default is 5 seconds.

member.name: P:Wolverine.AzureServiceBus.Internal.AzureServiceBusEndpoint.MaximumMessagesToReceive
member.summary: The maximum number of messages to receive in a single batch when listening in either buffered or durable modes. The default is 20.

member.name: P:Wolverine.AzureServiceBus.Internal.AzureServiceBusEndpoint.MaximumWaitTime
member.summary: The duration for which the listener waits for a message to arrive in the queue before returning. If a message is available, the call returns sooner than this time. If no messages are available and the wait time expires, the call returns successfully with an empty list of messages. Default is 5 seconds.

member.name: P:Wolverine.AzureServiceBus.Internal.AzureServiceBusEndpoint.Mapper
member.summary: If specified, applies a custom envelope mapper to this endp[oint

member.name: P:Wolverine.AzureServiceBus.Internal.AzureServiceBusQueue.DeadLetterQueueName
member.summary: Name of the dead letter queue for this SQS queue where failed messages will be moved
